metadata.title: Addendum to the guideline on the evaluation of medicinal products indicated for treatment of bacterial infections to address the clinical development of new agents to treat pulmonary disease due to Mycobacterium tuberculosis

rules.rule_text: It remains important to attempt to culture M. tuberculosis from appropriate baseline specimens to confirm organism identity and to assess susceptibility at least to the agents included in trial regimens.
rules.verbatim_quote: Currently it remains important to attempt to culture M. tuberculosis from appropriate baseline specimens in order to confirm organism identity and to assess susceptibility at least to the agents included in trial regimens.
rules.exceptions: In future it may be acceptable to replace culture with alternative non-culture-based (e.g. genomic) methods.

rules.rule_text: For new agents active against M. tuberculosis, in-vitro studies must evaluate activity against intracellular organisms and the effect of combining each new agent with other selected new or existing agents.
rules.verbatim_quote: for new agents active against M. tuberculosis it is relevant to evaluate activity against intracellular organisms and the effect of combining each new agent with other selected new or existing agents.

rules.rule_text: In-vitro pharmacodynamic models should be used to evaluate the contribution of each new agent within selected combination regimens, assess possible synergy or antagonism, and estimate the risk of selecting for resistant organisms.
rules.verbatim_quote: These models may be used to evaluate the contribution of each new agent when used within selected combination regimens, to assess the possible synergy or antagonism between the new agent and other selected agents (although the results may not necessarily predict the clinical efficacy of combined treatment regimens) and to estimate the risk of selecting for resistant organisms.

rules.rule_text: If rapid diagnostic tests are to be used to detect M. tuberculosis for patient enrolment, they must be specified in the protocol and used at central laboratories on screening visit specimens.
rules.source_section: 4.3 Microbiological data obtained during clinical trials
rules.verbatim_quote: If rapid diagnostic tests are to be used to detect M. tuberculosis, with or without detection of specific drug resistance, they should be specified in the protocol and used at all trial sites or testing should be conducted at central laboratories using screening visit specimens.

rules.rule_text: Isolates must be shipped to one or more designated central laboratories for confirmation of identity and susceptibility testing.
rules.source_section: 4.3 Microbiological data obtained during clinical trials
rules.verbatim_quote: Isolates should be shipped to one or more designated central laboratories for confirmation of identity and susceptibility testing.

rules.rule_text: The interpretation of negative cultures while patient is on therapy must be supported by adequate in-vitro studies to estimate the potential carry-over effects of drug concentrations in sputum using the selected processing and culture methods.
rules.source_section: 4.3 Microbiological data obtained during clinical trials — Detection of viable organisms during and post-therapy
rules.verbatim_quote: The interpretation of negative cultures obtained while the patient is still on therapy should be supported by adequate in-vitro studies to estimate the potential carry over effects of drug concentrations in sputum when using the selected processing and culture methods.

rules.rule_text: Protocols must explain procedures for handling contaminated cultures, including collection of fresh specimens when contamination is evident soon after collection.
rules.source_section: 4.3 Microbiological data obtained during clinical trials — Contaminated cultures
rules.verbatim_quote: Protocols should explain the procedures to be put in place in case of a contaminated culture either with a non-mycobacterial organism or with an organism belonging to a mycobacterial species other than M. tuberculosis. If contamination is evident soon after specimen collection and processing, a fresh specimen should be obtained if this is possible.

rules.rule_text: PK-PD analyses using PK and efficacy endpoint data from dose-finding trials (log drops in organism loads, SCC rates, time to SCC) must be conducted to support the regimen(s) assessed in pivotal trials. Sufficient PK data from pivotal trial patients must support exposure-response analysis.
rules.verbatim_quote: PK-PD analyses using PK and efficacy endpoint data from dose-finding trials (such as log drops in organism loads, SCC rates and time to SCC) should be conducted to support the regimen(s) assessed in pivotal trials. Furthermore, it is recommended that sufficient PK data should be obtained from patients in pivotal trials to support analyses of the exposure-response relationship.

rules.rule_text: Patient eligibility for TB trials must be based on both clinical (signs, symptoms, imaging) and laboratory evidence of active pulmonary tuberculosis.
rules.verbatim_quote: Patient eligibility should be based on both clinical (signs, symptoms and imaging) and laboratory evidence of active pulmonary tuberculosis.

rules.rule_text: Clinical trials for tuberculosis should employ directly observed treatment (DOT).
rules.source_section: 7.1 General considerations for trial design and analysis
rules.verbatim_quote: It is recommended that clinical trials should employ directly observed treatment (DOT).

rules.rule_text: Although double-blind design is preferred for pivotal TB studies, it is acknowledged this may not always be practical due to multiple co-administered agents. If open-label, measures must be in place to minimize unequal withdrawals and losses to follow-up.
rules.source_section: 7.1 General considerations for trial design and analysis
rules.verbatim_quote: Although a double-blind and double-dummy design is preferred, especially for pivotal studies, it is acknowledged that this may not always be a practical option due to the need to co-administer multiple agents... If a sponsor concludes that a double-blind design is not feasible it is important to consider the potential consequences of an unequal number of withdrawals from test and comparative regimens.

rules.rule_text: Protocols must address handling of patients found to be infected with organisms resistant to one or more assigned drugs after randomisation. These patients will usually need to be removed from the trial.
rules.source_section: 7.1 General considerations for trial design and analysis
rules.verbatim_quote: Handling of patients found to be infected with organisms that are resistant to one or more assigned drugs after they have been randomised and commenced therapy. These patients will usually need to be removed from the trial.
rules.exceptions: There may be exceptions, including retention of patients with rifampicin-susceptible but isoniazid-resistant organisms in some types of trial.

rules.rule_text: Confirmed sputum culture conversion (SCC) should be based on at least two (and preferably three) consecutive negative cultures of specimens obtained at timed intervals. The time to SCC should be based on the date of the first of the consecutive negative cultures.
rules.verbatim_quote: Confirmed SCC should be based on at least two (and preferably three) consecutive negative cultures of specimens obtained at timed intervals. The time to SCC may be based on the date of the first of the consecutive negative cultures.

rules.rule_text: Sustained SCC should be defined based on persistently negative cultures from the time of first SCC up to the last post-therapy visit.
rules.verbatim_quote: Sustained SCC should be defined based on persistently negative cultures from the time of first SCC up to the last post-therapy visit.

rules.rule_text: The definition of cure of pulmonary tuberculosis in clinical trials should require sustained SCC accompanied by documentation of improvement or resolution of clinical signs and symptoms associated with active tuberculosis.
rules.source_section: 7.2 Efficacy endpoints — Cure of pulmonary tuberculosis
rules.verbatim_quote: For the purposes of evaluating the efficacy of a regimen within a clinical trial the definition of cure of pulmonary tuberculosis should require sustained SCC (see above) accompanied by documentation of improvement or resolution of clinical signs and symptoms associated with active tuberculosis.

rules.rule_text: Relapse should be defined as the return of microbiologically confirmed tuberculosis with the same strain using appropriate typing methods. If relapse cannot be distinguished from new infection, the case should be counted as relapse in the primary analysis of efficacy.
rules.verbatim_quote: Relapse may be defined as the return of microbiologically confirmed tuberculosis with the same strain that caused the first episode of disease based on the use of appropriate typing methods. If it is not possible to distinguish relapse from new infection the case should be counted as a relapse in the primary analysis of efficacy.

rules.rule_text: In the primary analysis, all deaths should be counted as unfavourable outcomes. If deaths due to trauma are excluded, a sensitivity analysis counting all deaths as unfavourable must be planned.
rules.verbatim_quote: In the primary analysis the usual approach should be to count all deaths as unfavourable outcomes... If deaths due to trauma are excluded from the primary analysis there must be a sensitivity analysis planned in which all deaths are counted as unfavourable outcomes.

rules.rule_text: A short-term monotherapy trial is usually recommended for agents showing rapid bactericidal effect in vitro, unless in-vitro data suggest unacceptable risk of selecting for resistance when agent is given alone.
rules.verbatim_quote: Unless in-vitro data suggest that there is a potentially unacceptable risk of selecting for resistance if the new agent is administered alone, a short-term monotherapy trial of duration that takes into account the perceived risk of selecting for resistance is usually recommended at least for agents that show a rapid bactericidal effect in vitro.
rules.exceptions: Not recommended if in-vitro data suggest unacceptable risk of resistance selection.

rules.rule_text: In dose/regimen-finding trials, the primary analysis should be conducted in patients confirmed to be infected with organisms susceptible to all agents in their assigned regimen. An appropriate control group is required.
rules.verbatim_quote: The primary analysis should be conducted in those who are confirmed to be infected with organisms that are susceptible to all agents in their assigned regimen. There should be an appropriate control group.

rules.rule_text: For pivotal trials evaluating treatment-shortening regimens in susceptible TB, the primary analysis may be based on sustained SCC rates at a visit conducted at a fixed time post-randomisation that falls at least 6 months after the last dose of the longest regimen. Alternatively, it may be based on the incidence of unfavourable outcomes (failure to achieve SCC, relapses, and deaths).
rules.verbatim_quote: the primary analysis of efficacy may be based on sustained SCC rates determined at a visit conducted at a fixed time elapsed since randomisation and which falls at least 6 months after the last dose of the longest regimen included among the trial treatments. Alternatively, the primary endpoint could be defined as the incidence of unfavourable bacteriologic and clinical outcomes (i.e. counting all patients who fail to achieve sustained SCC, relapses and deaths as having an unfavourable outcome).

rules.rule_text: Secondary analyses should be conducted using all data collected up to a visit at 24 months after randomisation, comparing sustained SCC and cure rates between regimens.
rules.verbatim_quote: Secondary analyses should be conducted using all data collected up to a visit conducted at 24 months after randomisation. At this last visit, secondary analyses should compare the sustained SCC and cure rates between regimens.

rules.rule_text: Concomitant antibacterial therapy with agents of the same class as those in the trial regimen, or with known/potential efficacy against M. tuberculosis, should be avoided during the trial treatment period as it could interfere with culture results.
rules.verbatim_quote: antibacterial agents with known or potential efficacy against M. tuberculosis could interfere with culture results. In particular, antibacterial agents of the same class as those included in the trial regimens should be avoided.

rules.rule_text: For trials in patients with very few remaining treatment options, the primary comparison between test and control regimens should not occur before at least 6 months from start of therapy. Patients must be followed for at least 24 months from start and preferably at least 12 months after end of trial therapy.
rules.source_section: 7.3.3.2 Development of new agents within regimens that provide superior efficacy
rules.verbatim_quote: the primary comparison between test and control regimens should not occur before at least 6 months from start of therapy. It is essential that patients are followed to at least 24 months from the start of therapy and preferably for at least 12 months after the end of trial therapy.

rules.rule_text: An extrapolation of efficacy data from adults to paediatric patients aged approximately 10 years and older is acceptable. For children aged <10 years (especially <5 years), efficacy extrapolation is possible provided age-specific dose regimens are established using PK data from children with tuberculosis.
rules.source_section: 9 Considerations for special populations — Paediatric populations
rules.verbatim_quote: The presentation and treatment of pulmonary tuberculosis is similar in adults and paediatric patients aged from approximately 10 years so that an extrapolation of safety and efficacy data obtained from adults is acceptable... an extrapolation of efficacy data in adults to paediatric age groups is considered to be possible provided that appropriate age-specific dose regimens can be established using pharmacokinetic data obtained in children with tuberculosis

rules.rule_text: HIV-infected patients with sustained virological and cellular response to antiretroviral therapy may be included in TB trials along with HIV-negative individuals, provided efficacy is not expected to be adversely affected by additive toxicities and/or drug-drug interactions. Stratification by HIV status should be considered.
rules.source_section: 9 Considerations for special populations — HIV positive patients
rules.verbatim_quote: Sponsors may choose to evaluate new regimens in adequately treated HIV-infected patients separately or to include them in clinical trials along with HIV-negative individuals provided that the efficacy of test regimens is not expected to be adversely affected by factors such as additive toxicities and/or drug-drug interactions... consideration should be given to stratification by HIV status to achieve adequate numbers in each sub-group
